TIBET · LHASA TO NYINGCHI
Overview Highlights Itinerary Dining Hotels
Featured Theme Route

Sacred Lhasa & the Valleys of Nyingchi

A seven-to-eight-day Tibet introductory route through Potala views, Barkhor teahouses, monastery courtyards, lake water, forest valleys and snow-peak scale.

View the route Open daily meals Compare stays

Route Rhythm

Slow arrival · richer nature days

Core Areas

Lhasa · Nyingchi · Basum Lake

Best For

First-time Tibet travelers

Route Overview

Highland culture first, snowland nature next.

The route enters through Lhasa’s sacred-city neighborhoods and sweet tea houses, then moves into Nyingchi’s river valleys, forests, lakes and canyon views. The pace is deliberately spacious so every day has one clear experience and a practical reason to be there.

Day 1–3

Lhasa

Arrival, Potala skyline, Barkhor Street, Jokhang neighborhood and monastery layers.

Day 4

Transfer

The route shifts from sacred-city rhythm into the Niyang River valley.

Day 5–6

Nyingchi

Basum Lake, Lulang Forest, Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon and Namcha Barwa atmosphere.

Day 7/8

Closure

A low-intensity return or departure day keeps the full route steady.

Highlights

What the journey feels like

From Lhasa’s teahouses to Nyingchi’s lakes, forests and canyon views, the route moves from sacred-city life into softer highland nature.

Lhasa

Teahouse rhythm

Sweet tea and neighborhood pauses make Lhasa feel lived-in rather than only monumental.

Nyingchi

Lake and forest day

Basum Lake and Lulang offer softer highland scenery after Lhasa’s sacred-city intensity.

Grand Canyon

Snowland scale

The route closes with scale: river, canyon, clouds and the possibility of Namcha Barwa views.

Itinerary

A clear daily rhythm from Lhasa to Nyingchi

Each day keeps one main focus, so the journey feels rich without becoming physically dense.

Lhasa and Transfer

Arrival, old-city walks and monastery views at a measured highland pace.

Easy arrival · short walk · warm dinner · early rest

Courtyards · white walls · hillside perspectives

Niyang River valley · town check-in · rest

Nyingchi and Return

Valley forests, lake water, canyon views and a soft final departure.

Lake Water and Forests

Boardwalks, forest villages or stone-pot chicken

Day6

Snowland Scale

Canyon viewpoints · Namcha Barwa atmosphere

Day7/8

Return or Departure

A Gentle Closing Day

Low-intensity return · final tea-house stop · onward departure

Daily Dining

Route Dining Board

A gentle food rhythm from Lhasa teahouses to Nyingchi valley soups. Choose one day to view breakfast, lunch and dinner.

Sweet Tea Tibetan Noodles Yak Momo Butter Tea Stone-pot Chicken Mushroom Soup

Day 1 Meals

Lhasa Arrival Day

Warm, light and easy to digest — the first day is about settling into Lhasa without rushing.

¥70–140 / person

Breakfast

If you arrive early, start with Tibetan yogurt, plain bread or a cup of sweet tea near the hotel. Planning range: ¥20–35.

Lunch

Choose Tibetan noodles or a mild noodle soup around the outer Barkhor / Potala area. Planning range: ¥25–45.

Dinner

Keep dinner warm and simple: thenthuk, clear yak broth or vegetable-and-noodle soup. Planning range: ¥45–80.

Hotels

Stay choices that make the route easier.

In Lhasa, the right stay makes the first days calmer. In Nyingchi, the right base makes the lake, forest and canyon days easier.

Day 1–3 Base

Lhasa Base

For Potala views, Jokhang, Barkhor tea-house walks and the monastery day.

Stay near the old city rhythm

Practical Details

Tickets & Rides

Route stops, entry windows and public options are grouped here for quick planning. Local buses are listed only for places used in this route.

Bus routes are listed as available or commonly used options. For the day of travel, follow the current local transport information and reservation platforms.

The skyline landmark of Lhasa; this route uses the exterior view, with an interior visit as an optional upgrade.

Ticket

Exterior view: no ticket · Interior option: peak Route 1 ¥200 / Route 2 ¥100; low-season references from ¥100 / ¥50

Hours

09:00–16:00 · common last entry reference 15:00

Best for: First-time visitors · landmark skyline · optional palace interior

The spiritual center of the old city, surrounded by the Barkhor walking circuit.

Ticket

High season ¥85 · low season references from ¥35

Hours

Visitor window often planned around 11:30–17:30

Best for: Sacred-city atmosphere · short walk · cultural core

One of Lhasa’s major Gelug monasteries, known for courtyards and debate tradition.

Ticket

¥50

Hours

09:00–16:00 / 17:00 · debate window often 15:00–17:00

Best for: Monastery life · debate culture · lighter half-day

A large hillside monastery complex on the western edge of the city.

Ticket

¥60

Hours

09:00–16:00

Best for: Large monastery space · mountain-city views · history

Turquoise lake water, forest edges, island temple and a softer nature rhythm.

Ticket

Adult ticket + round-trip shuttle commonly listed around ¥165

Hours

09:00–18:30

Best for: Lake scenery · easy nature day · photography

Forest sea, meadow views and the best-known stone-pot chicken meal stop along the Nyingchi route.

Ticket

Route-view / meal stop: no fixed required ticket · paid forest or pasture areas only if selected

Hours

Daytime window commonly referenced as 07:00–18:00

Best for: Forest scenery · G318 views · stone-pot chicken meal stop

A canyon-and-peak day built around Namcha Barwa views and valley scale.

Ticket

Admission ¥150 · south-bank shuttle package commonly ¥240

Hours

08:30–19:00

Best for: Full scenic day · snow-peak views · canyon scale

Main city stop

Niangre Road No.1 Civil Aviation Office → LXA

04:30–19:30 · every 10–15 min · about 70 min · ¥30.

LXA → City

T3 Terminal, outside Gate 5

Arranged with inbound flights; leaves when full. Last bus matches the final inbound flight.

Liuwu Station → LXA

08:00 09:00 10:00 14:00 15:00 16:00

About 60 min · ¥30.

Potala area: Bus 1 / 13 / 14 / 89 reach the Potala Palace Square or nearby Beijing Road area. City fare reference: ¥1.
Jokhang / Barkhor: Bus 7 / 8 / 15 / 18 / 23 / 24 / 25 / 29 to Chongsaikang; Bus 1 / 11 / 12 / 17 to the old-town edge.
Sera Monastery: Bus 6 / 16 / 20 / 23 / 24 to Sera Monastery stop.
Drepung Monastery: Bus 16 / 17 / 24 / 25 to Drepung Monastery stop; walking uphill is still needed after the stop.
Sera ↔ Drepung direct line: first bus 07:00 · last bus 20:10 · about 30 minutes one way · stops: Sera Monastery → Aba Lin → Geological Team No.2 → Teachers College → Barku → Drepung Monastery.
TrainFromToTime
C437907:4511:333h48
C88108:1011:593h49
C438011:5515:443h49
C88212:2516:183h53

Useful for Day 4 transfer and Day 7/8 return. Main stops include Shannan, Jiacha, Langxian and Milin.

Basum Lake: Nyingchi Passenger Transport Center has buses to Basum Lake; distance about 118 km, ride about 2–2.5 h, fare reference ¥62.
Lulang: no dedicated public scenic shuttle is commonly listed; use a booked vehicle or a G318 corridor vehicle from Bayi town.
Grand Canyon / Pai Town: Bayi town buses to Pai Town are commonly referenced at 09:00 and 14:30; ride about 3 h, fare reference ¥60.

Travel Support

Prepared details that make the route feel smooth.

For international visitors, permit handling, scenic reservations, hotel reception arrangements and local route coordination are prepared as part of the travel support, so the journey stays focused on the experience.

Highland rhythm

Day 1 and Day 2 stay light, warm and steady.

Monastery etiquette

Respect clothing, photography boundaries and quiet visiting rules.

Weather A/B plan

Clear days favor peaks and reflections; misty days favor forests, villages and canyon atmosphere.

Dining cards

Allergies, vegetarian needs and beef/lamb preferences are easier with bilingual phrases.

Tibet Introductory Exploration Route

Lhasa · Barkhor Street · Tibetan teahouses · Nyingchi valleys · forests · lakes · snow-peak views
